task: Propose a novel small-molecule exercise mimetic for age-related muscle loss / sarcopenia, from public data
approach: Target-based drug design: identify the best-supported exerkine target, mine known chemical matter, design novel analogs, and validate computationally (properties, docking, ADMET, novelty).

steps.action: Enumerated exerkine/muscle candidate targets and pulled evidence from Open Targets (disease associations, tractability, safety), ChEMBL (chemical matter), and GTEx (tissue expression).
Scored 8 candidates across 7 weighted criteria (exerkine evidence, age-direction, druggability, chemical matter, structure availability, safety, muscle expression).
steps.result: APLNR/APJ selected (composite 4.90/5). Rationale: apelin is a bona fide exerkine that declines with age and reverses sarcopenia in mice (Vinel 2018); APJ is a structurally-characterized class A GPCR with rich agonist SAR and zero Open Targets safety events.

steps.action: Mined ChEMBL target CHEMBL1628481 (4,889 potent EC50 agonist rows) and BindingDB; retrieved reference agonists CMF-019, AMG 986, BMS-986224 from PubChem; cross-validated PDB co-crystal ligands by InChIKey.
Derived a 4-feature pharmacophore from the 7SUS orthosteric pocket: anionic anchor (acid to R168/K268), aromatic core (into Y/W/F cage), aryl wing, lipophilic tail.
Designed 15 analogs (D01-D15) by single-point modification of the CMF-019 G-protein-biased benzimidazole scaffold. D01 exactly reproduces CMF-019 (InChIKey match) as a construction control.
steps.note: An initial build had a SMILES ring-closure digit collision producing macrocyclic artifacts for two analogs; detected via 2D depiction + ring-size audit and rebuilt with collision-free numbering. All 15 verified as clean azabenzimidazoles with intact acid + core.

steps.action: Computed RDKit physicochemical panel + PAINS/Brenk alerts for all designs and references; hard-filtered on Lipinski/Veber/alerts/MW/logP.

Confirmed all shortlisted designs are new chemical entities: 0 hits in PubChem (exact+2D), ChEMBL (<=70% similarity), and ZINC22 (exact). Positive control: CMF-019 correctly retrieves ChEMBL neighbors.

Rigid-receptor docking with smina into 7SUS. Validated setup by redocking co-crystal ligand 8EH (crystal pose recovered at 1.36 A RMSD among top modes). Docked shortlist + references; analyzed salt-bridge and aromatic-cage contacts across all poses.
steps.result: D10 top score -9.4 kcal/mol (vs CMF-019 -8.3, AMG986 -8.8); D10 is the only design whose top-ranked pose satisfies the K268 salt bridge (2.96A) + 6/7 aromatic cage.

steps.action: Computed 5 drug-likeness rule sets (Lipinski, Veber, Egan, Ghose, Muegge), synthetic accessibility, and absorption/BBB heuristics.
steps.result: D10 passes all 5 rule sets (references fail 2-4 each); SA_score 3.32 (readily synthesizable); predicted peripherally-restricted (favorable for a muscle/cardiovascular target).

steps.action: Ranked shortlist by a transparent integrated score (drug-likeness 0.40 + docking 0.35 + pose quality 0.25).
steps.result: D10 selected as lead (0.834), ahead of D06 (0.659) and D13 (0.528).

limitations: Docking is rigid-receptor smina scoring — a triage tool, not a binding-affinity measurement; scores rank-order but do not quantitate.

No wet-lab validation: predicted agonism, functional selectivity (G-protein bias), and ADMET are computational hypotheses requiring in vitro confirmation (cAMP/beta-arrestin, calcium mobilization, microsomal stability).
G-protein bias is inferred from scaffold lineage (CMF-019), not predicted de novo.
SAR ladder demonstrates single-point rationale but potencies are not predicted — the design optimizes drug-likeness while preserving the pharmacophore, not measured EC50.
